codeReview.summary: The rerun fix adequately resolves the prior failure. Compared with the original failed commit `61659a747f4869b41b4ddb73da1eab8641252d95`, the fix commit removes the remaining forbidden `globals.css` utilities (`--border-accent`, `--color-border-accent`, `.glow-accent`, `--glow-green`, `.glow-green`), updates the stale assertions that had been codifying those tokens, and adds a dedicated enforcement suite covering both forbidden-token absence and the allowed atmospheric/status-dot exceptions. A repo review of `src/` found no remaining shipped definitions or component usages of those forbidden utilities beyond explanatory comments/tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library still contains stale design-token guidance that conflicts with the enforced monochrome-only UI rule. Future workers could reintroduce forbidden blue/green utilities by following the committed library docs instead of the mission AGENTS rules.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/test/vision/.factory/library/design-system.md:69` still documents `--glow-green`; `/Users/test/vision/.factory/library/tradebetter-exact-design-reference.md:829` still shows `--glow-green`; and `/Users/test/vision/.factory/library/tradebetter-comparison-evidence.md:49`, `:65`, and `:103` still describe `--border-accent` / blue focus borders even though the mission AGENTS color rules require `#455eff` to stay atmospheric-gradient-only and `#00ff00` to stay status-dot-only.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The recorded procedure evidence is not fully consistent with the frontend visual-work skill. The handoff says `followedProcedure=true` with no deviations, but the saved transcript skeleton for this session shows no `agent-browser` usage or browser-validation step even though the skill requires `agent-browser` for rendered user-facing changes and step 22 explicitly asks workers to exercise changed flows in the browser. This may indicate either a worker-reporting miss or that the skill needs a clearer exception for CSS-token-only fixes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill requirements: `/Users/test/vision/.factory/skills/frontend-experience-worker/SKILL.md` ('Required Skills' lists `agent-browser` as mandatory for every feature that changes a rendered user-facing flow, and step 22 requires browser exercise at `http://127.0.0.1:3100`). Handoff metadata: `/Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/handoffs/2026-03-23T15-58-37-068Z__redesign-purge-forbidden-blue-green-css-utilities__4cdb3832-c358-4ab9-b991-06d51b624c49.json:93-94` marks `followedProcedure: true` with no deviations. The corresponding transcript entry in `/Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/worker-transcripts.jsonl` for session `4cdb3832-c358-4ab9-b991-06d51b624c49` shows only file reads/searches/edits/test commands and no `agent-browser` invocation.

summary: Pass. The rerun directly fixes the prior blocker by deleting the remaining forbidden blue/green CSS utilities, updating stale assertions that had preserved them, and adding targeted enforcement tests so the purge is now covered. Shared-state follow-up: the library docs still contain outdated accent-token guidance, and the worker's recorded skill compliance does not show the browser-validation step that the frontend skill currently requires.
